Name: Noisy_Novel
Context: [Agent_1] is attempting to complete their novel today.
Desire: [Agent_1] wants to finish their novel undisturbed.
Belief: [Agent_1] believes that their neighbors, [Agent_3] and [Agent_2] will be considerate and quite.
Outcome: [Agent_2] constantly makes loud noise and distractions.
Action: [Agent_1] decides to knock on [Agent_2]'s door and confronts their neighbor about the noise.
Opposing Desire: [Agent_1] hopes they can complete their novel in a noisy environment because it will inspire him more.
Opposing Belief: [Agent_1] believes that their neighbors, [Agent_3] and [Agent_2] will be loud and noisy.
Opposing Outcome: [Agent_2] and [Agent_3] are quiet and peaceful today.

Name: Rainy_Party
Context: Someone suggests Cameron plan a barbecue with her friends.
Desire: Cameron wants to create a fun and memorable barbecue party.
Belief: Cameron believes the barbecue party will go smoothly and successfully.
Outcome: Gloria notices on the weather forecast that it is going to rain soon and told Cameron.
Action: Cameron leads all her friends to go from outside to her home to continue the party.
Opposing Desire: Cameron wishes to have a quiet evening alone.
Opposing Belief: Cameron believes his party will not be fun and successful and is better not thrown.
Opposing Outcome: Gloria notices that the weather is going to be perfect and told Cameron.
Opposing Action: Cameron stays outdoors to continue holding the party.

Name: Intro_Roommate
Context: Emily has recently moved into a co-living space.
Desire: Emily wants to get to know her new housemates better.
Belief: Emily believes that her housemates will be easy to get along with.
Outcome: Emily discovers that her housemates are quite introverted and prefer to keep to themselves.
Emotion: Emily feels disappointed and a bit isolated.
Action: Emily goes back to her own room (or goes out and takes a walk).
Opposing Desire: Emily prefers to maintain her privacy and distance from her housemates.
Opposing Belief: Emily thinks it's difficult to build a relationship with her roommates right after moving in and believes it should be done later.
Opposing Outcome: Emily's housemates are extremely friendly and frequently invite her to social activities.
Opposing Action: Emily accepts her housemates' invitation and stays at their room.

Name: Chaotic_Bar
Context: Jake is serving drinks at the local bar when a group of rowdy college students enter.
Desire: Jake wants to maintain a peaceful environment in his bar.
Belief: Jake thinks that if he talks to the students calmly, they will settle down.
Outcome: The students ignore Jake's request and continue to cause chaos.
Action: Jake leaves the bar to ask Gilbert for help.
Opposing Desire: Jake wishes to join in the fun and let loose for the night.
Opposing Belief: Jake believes that talking to the students is useless and will not solve the problem.
Opposing Outcome: The students think that Jake's words make sense and quiet down.
Opposing Action: Jake stays in the bar and starts dancing with the students.

Name: New_Barista
Context: Sarah ordered a cup of coffee from the new barista Amy at her regular cafe.
Desire: Sarah wants her coffee ready as soon as possible, since she is in a hurry.
Belief: Sarah believes that the new barista Amy can make the coffee quickly.
Outcome: Amy is still not very skilled at operating the coffee machine and hasn't been able to make the coffee properly.
Emotion: Sarah is a bit angry and very disappointed with the barista.
Action: Sarah walked over to the barista and canceled her coffee order.
Opposing Desire: Sarah doesn't mind when her coffee will be ready.
Opposing Belief: Sarah does not trust that the new barista can make good coffee.
Opposing Outcome: Amy quickly and perfectly made the coffee.
Opposing Emotion: Sarah was very relaxed and understanding of the barista.
Opposing Action: Sarah stayed in place and waited patiently.
